content.instruction: Choose the grammatically correct sentence for each question.

source.text: Subject-verb agreement is one of the most common grammar issues for English learners. The verb must agree with the subject in number — singular subject takes singular verb, plural subject takes plural verb. Be careful with tricky cases like 'each', 'everyone', 'a group of', and 'neither/nor'.

activities.instruction: Pick the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.

options.A: Each of the students have a book.
options.B: Each of the students has a book.
options.C: Each of the students having a book.

options.A: Neither John nor his friends is coming.
options.B: Neither John nor his friends are coming.
options.C: Neither John nor his friends am coming.

options.A: The team are winning the match.
options.B: The team is winning the match.
options.C: The team be winning the match.

options.A: Everyone in the office are happy today.
options.B: Everyone in the office is happy today.
options.C: Everyone in the office be happy today.
